strengths: Strong SQL and complex query experience
weaknesses: No explicit transaction surveillance/screening platform experience
No direct monitoring framework calibration experience
Few examples of control review or assurance participation
Limited explicit platform administration or model/rule testing
improvement_bullets: Optimized relational database queries to perform data reconciliation and validation, ensuring the integrity of datasets used for financial surveillance and monitoring frameworks.
Drafted technical documentation for complex data movement and lineage, ensuring cross-functional alignment during platform administration and system enhancement cycles.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to investigate data quality issues, coordinating remediation efforts and tracking corrective actions to closure within JIRA.
Supported internal control reviews by performing deep-dive data analysis, ensuring data adherence to business logic and regulatory standards for reporting.
Designed and executed rule performance testing via Python and SQL scripts to calibrate detection logic, minimizing false positives in automated monitoring workflows.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the AFC Systems Data Analyst opportunity with Partnership Employment. I am interested in this opportunity because of my deep commitment to data integrity and my desire to apply my analytical background to the critical field of financial crime compliance. What interests me about working for Partnership Employment is your reputation for maintaining robust and secure financial monitoring systems; this focus on data precision truly resonates with my own professional approach to technical problem-solving. I believe my expertise in complex database querying and my experience in data lineage and reconciliation would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have consistently approached data challenges with a focus on auditability and accuracy. In fact, while at Haven Technologies, I optimized relational database queries using window functions and CTEs to clean and reconcile insurance testing data, which improved overall data accuracy by 30%. This project required me to map complex data relationships and document them for stakeholders, which directly mirrors the requirements for supporting data governance and documentation at your firm. Ultimately, I know that the AFC Systems Data Analyst role at Partnership Employment would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in SQL and Python to the refinement of your transaction surveillance and screening platforms. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my data governance and reporting skills. I was tasked with replacing manual, error-prone workflows with automated, high-visibility dashboards that served as the primary source of truth for executive decision-making. By creating clear documentation and SOPs, I reduced operational bottlenecks by 25%, a mindset I am eager to apply to tracking corrective actions and assisting with platform administration at your company. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my data validation and reconciliation skills in working on the ongoing effectiveness of monitoring frameworks at Partnership Employment. I know that my background in ETL pipeline development and my attention to regulatory-grade data standards align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
